jobs.name: ao:jleechanclaw-30m-drive-to-7-green
jobs.prompt: Drive open PRs in jleechanorg/jleechanclaw toward 7-green autonomously. Each run: (1) check GH rate limits, (2) list open PRs via REST (`gh api repos/jleechanorg/jleechanclaw/pulls?state=open&per_page=100`), (3) for each PR compute age from created_at as Xh Ym (e.g. "2h 15m"), evaluate all 7-green conditions, and classify status as: ok (all criteria met or only non-blocking nits) or concerning (any blocking issue), (4) post a PR status line for EACH open PR: "PR #<N> — age: <X>h <Ym> — status: ok|concerning", then a brief blocker note if concerning, (5) if a PR is off-track with no AO session, spawn one via `ao spawn --claim-pr N -p jleechanclaw`, (6) summarize next actions (2-3 bullets max). If a PR is 7-green, post: "PR #N is green — awaiting human merge review". Use REST fallback when GraphQL rate-limited.

jobs.name: life:renew-car-registration-hourly

jobs.name: ao:skeptic-hourly-report
jobs.prompt: Hourly skeptic report for jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator. Check open PRs via REST (gh api repos/jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator/pulls?state=open&per_page=20). For each PR: compute age in hours from created_at, check CI/checks, CodeRabbit state, mergeable status, evidence and skeptic gate. Classify each PR as ON TRACK (all applicable green criteria met, or skeptic passed and only mechanical steps remain) or OFF TRACK (failing any required gate). For OFF TRACK PRs explain specifically what is wrong and the concrete action needed to fix it. Format for Slack with sections: 🟢 On Track (PR# age<Xh, short description of green gates), 🟡 Off Track - Actionable (PR# age<Xh, specific gate failing, exact fix needed), 🔴 Off Track - Blocked (PR# age<Xh, specific blocker requiring human/AO decision). Footer: next cron ETA. Keep concise (<=15 bullets), no markdown tables, prefer REST gh api. Include PR age in hours for every PR listed.

jobs.name: AO status report 5m
jobs.prompt:
Check the status of these 3 AO sessions and post a brief status report to the user:
1. ao-4592 (Skeptic Rule 14 PR) — in agent-orchestrator project
2. ao-4593 (Harness Phase 0) — in agent-orchestrator project  
3. jc-1822 (GitHub Budget Burn) — in jleechanclaw project

Run these commands:
```
export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm"; [ -s "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" ] && . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"
ao status 2>&1 | grep -E 'ao-4592|ao-4593|jc-1822'
```

For each session, also capture the last 10 lines of tmux output to see what the agent is actually doing:
```
tmux capture-pane -t bb5e6b7f8db3-ao-4592 -p -S -10 2>/dev/null | tail -8
tmux capture-pane -t bb5e6b7f8db3-ao-4593 -p -S -10 2>/dev/null | tail -8
tmux capture-pane -t bb5e6b7f8db3-jc-1822 -p -S -10 2>/dev/null | tail -8
```

Report:
- Session name, status (working/ready/exited), current activity
- If any session has exited or pushed to remote, report the PR/commit URL
- If all 3 sessions have completed, say "All sessions complete" and this cron can be considered done

jobs.name: ao-worker-notifier
jobs.prompt:
Check for completed AO worker sessions and post results to their originating Slack threads.

1. Run: python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/ao-session-notifier.py poll
2. If any results are returned (JSON array with session_id, channel, thread_ts, message, status), post each result to the corresponding Slack thread using the MCP Slack tools (mcp_slack_conversations_replies to read context, then post the message in-thread).
3. If no results (empty array []), do nothing — this is a quiet no-op cycle.
4. After posting all results, print a one-line summary: "Posted N results: [session_ids]"

Use Slack MCP tools to post messages in-thread. Format each post clearly with emoji status indicators (✅ completed, ⚠️ partial, ❌ failed).

jobs.name: ao-worker-status
jobs.prompt:
Run the AO worker status report script and deliver each status line to the right Slack thread.

Steps:
1. Run: `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/ao-status-report.py`
2. For each output line matching `POST:slack:<channel>:<thread_ts>:<message>`, use send_message to post to `slack:<channel>:<thread_ts>` with the message text.
3. If the output says "No active AO tasks tracked", do nothing (don't post).

The script auto-removes dead sessions from the tracker JSON, so you don't need to clean up.

Do NOT post any summary or commentary — just deliver the status lines.

jobs.name: babysit-wa-2248-dice-audit-fix
jobs.prompt:
You are a babysit poll for AO session wa-2248 (dice audit / dispatch fix, PR #7278). Your job is to monitor the worker and post a brief progress update to Slack every poll.

Worker task: dice audit / dispatch fix for issue #7277 (campaign i9xdU7P2bNoMpGqfLBHe, Itachi V3.1). Worker should: (1) branch fix/7277-dice-audit-dispatch off origin/main in a new worktree, (2) add structured d20 audit fields + recognize code_execution as a valid dice tool in mvp_site/dice.py, (3) follow TDD with /es evidence (real local server + real LLM + Firestore proof), (4) push and reply with PR URL.

Each poll, run:
```
python3 ~/.hermes/skills/ao-babysit/scripts/babysit.py poll \
  --session wa-2248 \
  --slack-channel C0AH3RY3DK6 \
  --slack-thread-ts 1780695000.488709 \
  --task-summary "dice audit / dispatch fix for issue #7277 (campaign i9xdU7P2bNoMpGqfLBHe, Itachi V3.1). Worker should: (1) branch fix/7277-dice-audit-dispatch off origin/main in a new worktree, (2) add structured d20 audit fields + recognize code_execution as a valid dice tool in mvp_site/dice.py, (3) follow TDD with /es evidence (real local server + real LLM + Firestore proof), (4) push and reply with PR URL."
```

If the session is dead but the PR has the work complete, post a single end-of-life message and self-terminate. If the worker is alive and on-task, do not post a long message — the script handles that. If the worker is stuck/dead mid-work, post a death alert with concrete remediation steps.

Run the WorldArchitect Daily Level Up + Daily Dice watcher. It reads the latest "[GCP Cron] Daily Level Up Test" and "[GCP Cron] Daily Dice Audit" emails and posts structured pass/fail notices in #worldai (C0AH3RY3DK6). The script is idempotent on job + email run_date so re-runs are safe.

jobs.prompt:
You are babysitting AO worker session wa-2258 (bead: rev-wva6b, GH issue #7318).

Worker location: worktree /Users/jleechan/.worktrees/worldarchitect/wa-2258, branch fix/7318-exp-session-header, project worldarchitect.

Steps:

If worker has exited (no active thinking indicator, prompt visible, possibly a commit/PR mentioned): check `cd /Users/jleechan/.worktrees/worldarchitect/wa-2258 && git log --oneline -3` and `gh pr list --repo jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai --head fix/7318-exp-session-header --state all --json number,url,title`. Do not poll ao status — tmux capture-pane is faster and shows ground truth.

Worker branch target: fix/investigate-godmode-l6-revert (off origin/main = ff979f9f9d)
Twin campaign: UuOl2cnEZwjl3AfQavvs (jleechantest@gmail.com) at https://mvp-site-app-dev-i6xf2p72ka-uc.a.run.app/game/UuOl2cnEZwjl3AfQavvs
Source campaign (READ-ONLY): NFBaxQ3mIUe17UlAAGlE (jleechan@gmail.com)
Bead brief: /tmp/wa-godmode-l6-bead-brief.md
Known override line: mvp_site/rewards_engine.py:1007-1053 block_unauthorized_level_mutations

Run these checks in order, then post a 1-line update to the thread:

1. `cd /Users/jleechan/.worktrees/worldarchitect/wa-2289 && git log --oneline -3` — confirm the worker is on `fix/investigate-godmode-l6-revert` and on top of ff979f9f9d. If it's still on `feat/rev-xe0e3` and hasn't switched, send an `ao send wa-2289` reminder with the branch-steer file content.

2. `cd /Users/jleechan/.worktrees/worldarchitect/wa-2289 && git diff --stat HEAD` — see if the worker has started writing files. If 0 lines changed after 10+ minutes, send a nudge via `ao send wa-2289`.

3. `cd /Users/jleechan/.worktrees/worldarchitect/wa-2289 && ls -la evidence/godmode-l6-twin/ 2>/dev/null` — see if the worker has started the twin repro. Empty or missing dir = nudge.

4. `ps -ef | grep -E "tmux.*wa-2289|wa-2289" | grep -v grep` — confirm the tmux session is alive.

5. Tail cloud logs to see if the worker has actually been hitting the dev preview: `timeout 15 gcloud logging read 'resource.type=cloud_run_revision AND resource.labels.service_name=mvp-site-app-dev AND jsonPayload.campaign_id="UuOl2cnEZwjl3AfQavvs" AND timestamp>="$(date -u -v-10M +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"' --limit=20 --format='value(textPayload)' 2>&1 | head -30`. If zero hits in 10 minutes, the worker hasn't run the repro yet — nudge it.

6. Post a 1-line update to the Slack thread (use mcp__slack__conversations_add_message with channel_id=C09GRLXF9GR and thread_ts=1781139255.231799) in this format:

   - If the worker has finished (evidence file written, PR comment posted), say "DONE — task complete" and EXIT cleanly (do not keep polling).

Stall detection: if the worker has been silent for 2 consecutive checks (10+ min) with no file changes and no log activity, send a strong nudge via `ao send wa-2289 -f /tmp/nudge.txt` with content like: "You're idle. Re-read /tmp/wa-godmode-l6-bead-brief.md Step 3 (real-LLM repro on twin) and execute it. If blocked, post the blocker to the Slack thread." Then do not post a Slack message — just wait for the next tick.

NEVER spawn additional AO workers from this cron. NEVER delete the worker. NEVER modify the bead.
